name: Sunset
description: The science, beauty, and superstition of the day's end.

questions.q: Why are sunsets often red and orange?
choices: Rayleigh scattering filters out shorter (blue) wavelengths
The sun cools as it sets

source.notes: Light passes through more atmosphere at low angles, scattering blue away.

questions.q: What color are sunsets on Mars typically?

source.original_a: Colors are typically hues of blue
source.notes: Caused by Mie scattering off Martian dust — opposite to Earth's red sunsets.

questions.q: What is the period right after sunset called?
What are the three stages of twilight in order from brightest to darkest?

source.notes: Civil ends at 6° below; Nautical at 12°; Astronomical at 18°.

questions.q: How many degrees below the horizon must the sun be for it to be officially 'night'?

source.original_a: When the Sun is 18 degrees below the horizon

questions.q: On which date is the latest sunset in the Northern Hemisphere?

source.notes: Latest sunset is a few days AFTER the solstice due to the equation of time.

questions.q: On which date is the earliest sunset in the Northern Hemisphere?

source.notes: Roughly two weeks BEFORE the solstice — equation of time at work.

questions.q: Where does the Sun set on the equinoxes (March 20 / Sept 22)?
What is the green flash?
choices: A brief flash of green light at the moment of sunset

source.notes: Caused by atmospheric refraction; visible at sea or with a clear horizon.

questions.q: What is the 'magic hour' (or 'golden hour') in photography?
choices: The first hour after sunrise / last before sunset

source.notes: Soft, warm, low-angle light prized by photographers.

questions.q: Which natural event can sometimes produce especially vivid red sunsets for months?

source.notes: Stratospheric ash from Krakatoa (1883) and Pinatubo (1991) created famously red sunsets.

questions.q: Which 1950 noir film features the line 'I am big. It's the pictures that got small'?

source.notes: Directed by Billy Wilder; Norma Desmond's famous line.

questions.q: Which California boulevard runs through Hollywood and is famous for its sunset views?

source.notes: Runs from downtown LA to the Pacific.

questions.q: Which 1971 song by The Kinks describes Waterloo at sunset?
Which Caribbean island is known as 'Land of the Sleeping Giant' with vibrant sunsets at Negril's Seven Mile Beach?

source.notes: Negril is famed for cliff-jumping and sunsets at Rick's Cafe.

questions.q: Which Greek island village is famous for one of the world's most photographed sunsets?

source.notes: Crowds gather at Oia Castle daily.

questions.q: Why do Polar Circles experience days where the sun never sets in summer?

source.notes: The 'midnight sun.' Above 66.5° latitude in either hemisphere.

questions.q: Which famous Florida island is known for green-flash sunsets and was painted by Audubon?

source.notes: Mallory Square's nightly sunset celebration is a Key West tradition.

questions.q: What does Cat Stevens (Yusuf) sing about waiting for in his 1971 song 'Moonshadow'?
Which atmospheric phenomenon causes the sun to actually be visible after it has geometrically set?

source.notes: Atmospheric refraction lifts the apparent position by ~½°—about a sun-diameter at the horizon.
